content.problemStatement:
You are given an array CCC of size NNN. You arrange the elements of the array CCC in a circle in clockwise manner.

You can perform the following two types of operations **exactly** (N−2)(N-2)(N−2) times in total:


Operation 111: Select two adjacent elements XXX and YYY, and replace XXX and YYY with XXX ∣\mid∣ YYY.
Operation 222: Select two adjacent elements XXX and YYY, and replace XXX and YYY with XXX &\&& YYY.
Here ∣\mid∣ and &\&& denote the bitwise OR and bitwise AND respectively.

Find the **maximum** difference possible between the two remaining elements after (N−2)(N-2)(N−2) operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, the size of CCC.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,…,CNC_1,C_2,\ldots ,C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** difference possible between the two remaining elements after (N−2)(N-2)(N−2) operations.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Consider the following 222 operations:

Do operation 111 for C4C_4C4​ and C1C_1C1​ (C1C_1C1​ and C4C_4C4​ are adjacent). Here, C4∣C1=3∣2=3C_4 \mid C_1 = 3 \mid 2 = 3C4​∣C1​=3∣2=3. Thus, the array becomes, C=[3,0,1]C=[3,0,1]C=[3,0,1]. Note that, we can also write CCC as [0,1,3][0,1,3][0,1,3].
Do operation 222 for C2C_2C2​ and C3C_3C3​ in the updated array (C2C_2C2​ and C3C_3C3​ are adjacent). Here, C2&C3=0&1=0C_2 \& C_3 = 0 \&1 = 0C2​&C3​=0&1=0. Thus, the array becomes, C=[3,0]C = [3, 0]C=[3,0].

The difference of the remaining elements is ∣3−0∣=3|3-0|=3∣3−0∣=3. It can be shown that this is the maximum possible difference after 222 operations.
Test case 222: Consider the following 222 operations:

Do operation 111 for C2C_2C2​ and C3C_3C3​ (C2C_2C2​ and C3C_3C3​ are adjacent). Here, C2∣C3=2∣5=7C_2 \mid C_3 = 2 \mid 5 = 7C2​∣C3​=2∣5=7. Thus, the array becomes, C=[1,7,6]C=[1, 7, 6]C=[1,7,6].
Do operation 222 for C1C_1C1​ and C3C_3C3​ in the updated array (C1C_1C1​ and C3C_3C3​ are adjacent). Here, C1&C3=1&6=0C_1 \& C_3 = 1 \& 6 = 0C1​&C3​=1&6=0. Thus, the array becomes, C=[7,0]C = [7, 0]C=[7,0].

The difference of the remaining elements is ∣7−0∣=7|7-0|=7∣7−0∣=7. It can be shown that this is the maximum possible difference after 222 operations.
